request.question: Which actress that was a member of South Korean girl group is co-staring in Unexpected Love?

title: Krystal Jung
Black Pearl (K-Pop Group)
Jewelry (band)
Unexpected Love (2017 film)
Kiss (Dara song)
Kim Chung-ha
D.Holic
Kwon So-hyun
GP Basic
Lina (South Korean singer)
sentences: Chrystal Soo Jung (born October 24, 1994), better known by the mononym Krystal, is an American singer and actress based in South Korea.
She debuted in 2009 as a member of South Korean girl group f(x) and has further participated in S.M. Entertainment's project group S.M.
The Ballad.
Aside from group activities, she has also participated in various television dramas such as "" (2011–12), "The Heirs" (2013), "My Lovely Girl" (2014), and "The Bride of Habaek" (2017).
Black Pearl (Hangul: 블랙펄) was a South Korean girl group official made up of Oh Nami, Hwl Eun, Young Joo, and Mikka.
When the group debuted in 2007 they marketed as the new girl group to dominate the girl group charts in the following years of 2007 and 2008 alongside some artist Wonder Girls, and Kara.
They were also known to follow the steps of SG Wannabe, SeeYa, and M2M.
In 2009 Mikka left due to personal reasons.
New member Jung Min was added by 2010 or 2011.
As for the 2010 and therefore the group only consisted of the following 4 until their disbandment in 2012.
Jewelry (Korean: 쥬얼리 ) was a South Korean girl group formed in 2001 by Star Empire Entertainment.
The group was the longest-running Korean girl group (with more than 2 members) until their disbandment in 2015, surpassing super-group Baby V.O.X.
Unexpected Love (also anown as: "Flying Kite" / "Bi Zui!
Ai Ba" / "Biyeon") is an upcoming Chinese-South Korean romantic comedy film directed by Si-Heup Seong and starring Zhang Yixing and Krystal Jung.
"Kiss" (Korean: 입맞춤) is a dance single performed by South Korean idol singer Sandara Park, a visual and member of the girl group 2NE1.
The song was used as part of a promotional campaign for Cass Beer.
It features the vocals of her co-member, CL of South Korean girl group 2NE1.
The song was released as her first official single in South Korea and was later included in the album "To Anyone".
Kim Chung-ha (born Kim Chan-mi on February 9, 1996), better known by the mononym Chungha, is a South Korean singer.
She is best known for finishing fourth in Mnet's girl group survival show "Produce 101" and as a former member of the now disbanded South Korean girl group I.O.I.
D.Holic (Hangul: 디홀릭) was a South Korean girl group formed by Star Road Entertainment (formerly H.Mate Entertainment) in 2014 with five members.
Nine left the group in August 2015, due to personal reasons, and was replaced by new member, Hwajung.
In July 2016, it was revealed through teasers that Danbee and Duri had decided to leave the group, new member, EJ, was added to the line-up.
In February 2017, it was confirmed through a performance that members Hami and Hwajung had departed from the group.
They were temporary replaced with new members, Nayoung and Youjin, although they never officially made their debut.
In July 2017, EJ announced that she would be leaving the group to pursue a modelling career.
The group informally disbanded after the departure of all but one member, with the aim of re-debuting the remaining member, Rena, into a new girl group within a year.
The group has released one mini-album: "Chewy" (2015) and three single albums: "D.Holic Dark With Dignity" (2014), "Murphy & Sally" (2015), and "Color Me Rad" (2016).
Kwon So-hyun (born August 30, 1994), is a South Korean singer and actress.
She was known as a member of the South Korean girl group 4Minute, under Cube Entertainment.
She is also a former member of the South Korean girl group, Orange.
Before June 15, 2016 (end of her contract with Cube), Sohyun left 4Minute and the record label along with members Nam Ji-hyun, Heo Ga-yoon, and Jeon Ji-yoon.
GP Basic (Korean: 지피 베이직 ) was a South Korean girl group who debuted on August 15, 2010.
At the time of their debut, they were considered to be the "youngest" South Korean girl group with an average age of below 13.5.
The age of their youngest member, Janey, caused controversy followed by the debut of a much younger girl group, G-story, who were all below 10 years old.
This led to new broadcasting regulations in South Korea.
Lee Ji-yeon (born February 18, 1984), better known by her stage name Lina is a South Korean singer and musical actress.
She debuted in 2002 as a member of South Korean R&B duo Isak N Jiyeon and disbanded in 2004.
She later re-debut as a member of South Korean girl group The Grace in 2005 and the group currently being inactive.
She currently active as a musical actress.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Produce a compact, structured first-hop summary that helps the next agent fetch the missing second-hop facts. The summary must be one labeled block with these four short sections (each 1–4 lines):

1) Evidence found — For each concrete target term or name mentioned in the passages, list the target and which passage(s) mention it, and quote a short verbatim excerpt (≤15 words) or any exact numeric/date token from the passage. Preserve the quoted text and any numeric/date formatting exactly as it appears in the passages. Include passage identifier(s) exactly as given (title or index).

2) Ambiguities — Enumerate any names or terms that correspond to multiple candidate pages or where the passages return multiple variants or conflicting values. For each ambiguous item, list the candidate labels or note the conflict using only tokens from the passages where possible.

3) Bridge / missing fact — State exactly which relation(s) or fact(s) needed to answer the question are not present or are unresolved in the provided passages (e.g., birth date, publication year, author of adaptation, founding year, award count). Be explicit and concise.

4) Retrieval direction — Provide 2–4 concrete next-hop retrievals: specific page titles or disambiguation pages, or precise search queries the next agent should run to fetch the missing bridge facts. Use actionable, precise phrasing (page title or quoted query). End this section with an explicit deferral of the final answer when the passages do not unambiguously supply all required facts.

Formatting and behavior rules:
- Produce only the single field `summary` as the output. The `summary` text must follow the four labeled sections above.
- Always quote verbatim evidence (names, short excerpts, dates, numbers) exactly as in the passages; do not reformat numeric/date tokens.
- Do NOT produce a final answer here unless every required fact to resolve the question is present and unambiguous in the provided passages (i.e., each named entity corresponds to a single candidate and the needed relation value is explicitly present). If facts are unambiguous and complete, you may state that and include a concise final conclusion in the `summary`; otherwise explicitly defer (for example: "Final answer deferred pending retrieval of the targets below.").
- If the natural summary would otherwise be a single bare token or name, append a one-line bridge clause that identifies the missing relation and at least one retrieval target rather than ending with the bare token.
- Keep phrasing concise and factual; favor short lines, source-linked evidence excerpts, and explicit next-step instructions. Do not attempt to fetch pages yourself—only list retrieval targets/queries.

Example structure to follow in the `summary` field (use passage text and titles exactly):
Evidence found: ...
Ambiguities: ...
Bridge / missing fact: ...
Retrieval direction: ...

End of instructions.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Required output format and constraints:
- Produce exactly one field named `query`. Its value must be a single short, keyword-style search string (no full sentences, no explanations, no assertions, no user-facing clarifications).
- The query must be terse (preferably <= 12 words) and contain no clause punctuation or sentence grammar (no question marks, no leading "who/what is", no verbs forming assertions).
- Use only tokens that are visible in `question` and/or `summary_1`, except you may insert at most one minimal neutral connector token to target bridging evidence (allowed connector tokens include, but are not limited to: artist, album, director, who directed, biography, introduction, genre, regular season). Do not invent new entity names or facts.
- When the inputs present multiple entities or candidate items that must be linked (for example two titles, two names, or an anchor title plus a candidate), include both entity tokens from the inputs and a single neutral connector token between or adjacent to them to indicate the evidence bridge to retrieve (e.g., "EntityA EntityB director").
- If the question or summary includes structured tokens relevant to the bridge (years, league IDs, edition names), include those tokens plus a concise cue (for example year + league + "regular season") as part of the short query.
- You may perform light normalization of obvious misspellings in entity names or technical terms drawn from the visible inputs (fix clear typos), but do not invent alternative candidate entities or assert corrected identities beyond the token-level fix.
- If it is not possible to form a fully disambiguating query from visible tokens, still emit the best compact retrieval query possible using only visible tokens and an allowed connector; do not ask the user for clarification at this step. (Downstream retrieval will be attempted first; user clarification is allowed only after retrieval fails.)

Produce only the `query` field value (the single short search string) and nothing else.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Instructions for producing `summary` (single text field only):
- Output exactly one concise summary string (no extra fields, labels, or metadata). Keep the summary to 1–2 short sentences unless the evidence forces a single-token identification (see below).
- Primary goal: integrate the first-hop identity in `context` with facts found in `passages` so the final answer agent has the explicit, supported linkage it needs.

When the question requires linking the context entity to information in the passages and the passages supply the bridging fact:
- Produce a concise (1–2 sentence) integrated summary that (1) names the context entity (using the form present in `context`), (2) names the supporting entity/attribute found in `passages` (using the form present in the inputs), and (3) states the explicit relation or fact (membership, creator attribution, partnership, founding/establishment, award, etc.) exactly as supported by the inputs. Use only facts present in `context` and `passages` and avoid introducing any unsupported details.

Name and attribution formatting:
- If a canonical multi-token name (given + family name or full official name) appears in the inputs, use that full form in the summary; do not truncate to a surname or partial name.
- For questions about creator/author/credit, prefer the person-name attribution as given in the inputs over returning the title of a work or event.

Identification requests:
- If the question explicitly asks for a single identification (for example, asks "which X" or otherwise requests a named token or label), return exactly the canonical name(s) as presented in the passages. If multiple valid names are requested, return them comma-separated. Do not add explanatory clauses, relational fragments, or extra text in these cases—only the canonical name(s).

When evidence is missing or ambiguous:
- If the passages do not supply a clear bridging fact, state that succinctly (e.g., "No supporting evidence in the provided passages" or "Unclear from the provided sources"). Retain uncertainty rather than asserting unsupported facts.

Conciseness and fidelity constraints:
- Keep the summary short and factual (1–2 sentences). Do not expand into long biographies, exhaustive lists, or unrelated details.
- Do not assert facts that are not present in `context` and `passages`.
- Avoid long verbatim copying of passage text, but short verbatim names or phrases necessary for fidelity are allowed.
- Treat the `context` field as the primary first-hop identity to be linked to `passages` when integrating evidence.

Produce the `summary` now based only on the supplied `question`, `context`, and `passages`.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Use summary_1 and summary_2 as the sole evidential basis for the final answer; give greatest weight to summary_2 (the later, post-retrieval integrated evidence). Do not introduce facts from outside these summaries.

Output rules (apply in order):

1) If summary_1 and summary_2 clearly and unambiguously agree on the same concise answer, set the value of `answer` to exactly one minimal string and nothing else:
   - For a binary yes/no conclusion, emit the single lowercase token: yes or no.
   - For any other concise label, emit that label exactly as it appears in the summaries (trim leading/trailing whitespace and strip any surrounding punctuation or wrapper syntax such as surrounding parentheses, quotes, or JSON-like braces). Do not add words, sentences, punctuation, quotes, brackets, or any other surrounding text.

2) Preserve multi-token labels: if any visible summary contains a multi-token form of the answer (for example a full personal name, a multi-word company or place), output that full multi-token form verbatim. Only shorten a label if no multi-token form appears in either summary.

3) Company/developer preference: when the question requests a company or developer and the summaries show a subordinate studio together with an explicit parent/publisher label (e.g., in parentheses or with phrasing like "published by"), choose the parent/publisher name as the single minimal answer string. If no parent/publisher is explicitly indicated, choose the best-supported developer/studio name from the summaries.

4) If the summaries conflict or are ambiguous, do NOT apply the unanimous-agreement rule in (1). Instead, use summary_2 as the primary basis and emit the minimal concise string supported by summary_2, applying rules (2) and (3) as relevant. If summary_2 provides no concise supported answer, set `answer` to the minimal explicit token "unknown".

Formatting constraints (must always be followed):
- Produce exactly one field: `answer`.
- The value of `answer` must be a single minimal string (no surrounding text, punctuation, explanation, or structural wrappers).
- Do not output extra fields, labels, or markup.

Additional guidance:
- If summary_1 describes an earlier unresolved bridge but summary_2 provides a resolved, supported conclusion, use the conclusion from summary_2.
- If a concise answer string can be drawn verbatim from the summaries, prefer that verbatim text over paraphrase.

End.

response.hop1_query: Which actress that was a member of South Korean girl group is co-staring in Unexpected Love?
hop1_docs: Ryu Hyo-young | Ryu Hyoyoung (born April 22, 1993), better known by the mononym Hyoyoung, is a South Korean actress, model, and rapper.  She was a member of the South Korean co-ed group Coed School and the girl group F-ve Dolls.
Lee Soomi | Lee Soomi (; born March 3, 1989) is a South Korean singer, dancer, rapper, and actress.  She is a former member of girl groups SeeYa, F-ve Dolls, and coed group Coed School.

Shoo (singer) | Yoo Soo-young or Shū Kunimitsu (born 23 October 1981), better known as Shoo, is a South Korean singer and musical actress.  She is best known as a member of South Korean girl group S.E.S..  In 2010, she released her debut solo extended play, "Devote One's Love."

Gain (singer) | Son Ga-in (born September 20, 1987), better known mononymously as Gain, is a South Korean singer, actress and entertainer.  She is best known as a member of the South Korean girl group Brown Eyed Girls and for her appearances alongside Jo Kwon from 2AM for TV shows "We Got Married" and "All My Love".  As a solo artist she has released six EPs.

Lina (South Korean singer) | Lee Ji-yeon (born February 18, 1984), better known by her stage name Lina is a South Korean singer and musical actress.  She debuted in 2002 as a member of South Korean R&B duo Isak N Jiyeon and disbanded in 2004.  She later re-debut as a member of South Korean girl group The Grace in 2005 and the group currently being inactive.  She currently active as a musical actress.

Yura (South Korean singer) | Kim Ah-young (born November 6, 1992), better known by her stage name Yura, is a South Korean singer and actress.  She is best known as a member of the South Korean girl group Girl's Day.

Krystal Jung | Chrystal Soo Jung (born October 24, 1994), better known by the mononym Krystal, is an American singer and actress based in South Korea.  She debuted in 2009 as a member of South Korean girl group f(x) and has further participated in S.M. Entertainment's project group S.M.  The Ballad.  Aside from group activities, she has also participated in various television dramas such as "" (2011–12), "The Heirs" (2013), "My Lovely Girl" (2014), and "The Bride of Habaek" (2017).
response.summary_1:
Evidence found:
- Ryu Hyo-young — [1] "the girl group F-ve Dolls."
- Lee Soomi — [2] "former member of girl groups SeeYa, F-ve Dolls"
- Shoo (Yoo Soo-young) — [3] "member of South Korean girl group S.E.S.."
- Gain (Son Ga-in) — [4] "member of the South Korean girl group Brown Eyed Girls"
- Lina (Lee Ji-yeon) — [5] "member of South Korean girl group The Grace"
- Yura (Kim Ah-young) — [6] "member of the South Korean girl group Girl's Day."
- Krystal Jung — [7] "member of South Korean girl group f(x)"

Ambiguities:
- Name variants: "Ryu Hyo-young | Ryu Hyoyoung" (passage [1])
- Multiple personal names/aliases: "Yoo Soo-young or Shū Kunimitsu" for Shoo (passage [3])
- Alternate romanizations: "Chrystal Soo Jung" vs "Krystal Jung" (passage [7])
- Stage name vs legal name: "Lina | Lee Ji-yeon" (passage [5])

Bridge / missing fact:
- No passage mentions "Unexpected Love" or any film titled "Unexpected Love".
- Missing fact: which named actress (e.g., Krystal, Yura, Ryu Hyo-young, etc.) "co-starring in Unexpected Love" (cast membership / co-star relation) is not present.

Retrieval direction:
- Open the film/cast page: "Unexpected Love (film)" or run query: "Unexpected Love cast"
- Check candidate filmographies: "Krystal Jung" page (filmography section)
- Check candidate filmographies: "Yura (South Korean singer)" page (filmography)
- Check candidate filmographies: "Ryu Hyoyoung" page (filmography) or "Ryu Hyo-young"
Final answer deferred pending retrieval of the targets above.
hop2_docs: Unexpected Love (2017 film) | Unexpected Love (also anown as: "Flying Kite" / "Bi Zui!  Ai Ba" / "Biyeon") is an upcoming Chinese-South Korean romantic comedy film directed by Si-Heup Seong and starring Zhang Yixing and Krystal Jung.
Jessica & Krystal | Jessica & Krystal is an OnStyle variety TV show starring Korean American sisters Jessica Jung, former member of Girls' Generation and Krystal Jung of f(x).

Krystal Jung | Chrystal Soo Jung (born October 24, 1994), better known by the mononym Krystal, is an American singer and actress based in South Korea.  She debuted in 2009 as a member of South Korean girl group f(x) and has further participated in S.M. Entertainment's project group S.M.  The Ballad.  Aside from group activities, she has also participated in various television dramas such as "" (2011–12), "The Heirs" (2013), "My Lovely Girl" (2014), and "The Bride of Habaek" (2017).

My Lovely Girl | My Lovely Girl () is a 2014 South Korean television series starring Jung Ji-hoon (Rain), Krystal Jung, Kim Myung-soo, and Cha Ye-ryun.  Having the K-pop industry as the backdrop, the drama tells the story of two pained individuals who find healing through music.  It aired on SBS from September 17 to November 6, 2014 on Wednesdays and Thursdays at 21:55 for 16 episodes.
Love It Away | "Love It Away" is the fifth single from the album "Make Some Noise" by Krystal Meyers, released in the United States and Japan in 2008.

Kostas Krystallis | Kostas Krystallis (Greek: Κώστας Κρυστάλλης ; 1868–1894) was a Greek author and poet, representative of 19th century Greek pastoral literature.  He was born an Ottoman subject in Epirus, but escaped to Greece after being denounced to the authorities for writing a patriotic collection of poetry.  Krystallis initially wrote his works in archaic language, but after 1891 he adopted the vernacular (Demotic) Greek language and became influenced by the New Athenian school.  He was a pictorial writer, with a love to nature, while most of his work was based on traditional folk poetry.

Huh Jung | Huh Jung (born 1981) is a South Korean film director and screenwriter.  Huh directorial debut - a low-budget horror-thriller "Hide and Seek" (2013) starring Son Hyun-joo, was an unexpected hit in 2013 with 5.6 million admissions.  His second feature - also a horror film, titled "The Mimic", stars actress Yum Jung-ah and is scheduled to release in 2017.
response.summary_2: Krystal Jung, a member of the South Korean girl group f(x), is co-starring in the film Unexpected Love.
